supersedes: audits/api-webhooks-shape-snapshot-2026-05-29.json (additive — old file retained; only the assets[] token form is new)
naming_law: Public event fields use price_at_call / call, never price_at_signal / signal. No outcome_* ever (Data Integrity).

request_keys.assets: OPTIONAL string[] — each entry is a coin symbol (e.g. "BTC") OR a `top:N` token where N is 1-100 (e.g. "top:25"). null/empty = all assets. Tokens + coins may be mixed; multiple tokens allowed; match = ANY entry hits. NEW in C4.

assets_token_contract.semantics: top:N subscribes to any coin in the event venue's current top-N-by-open-interest set, resolved per-event against the event's own exchange (getTopCoinSet). Only the 5 PROMOTED venues (HL/BINANCE/BYBIT/OKX/BITGET) resolve tokens; shadow-venue events never match a token (fail-quiet). A universe-fetch failure leaves the token non-matching — NO delivery storm, NO alert.
assets_token_contract.stored_as: verbatim (no migration; assets already stored as a string array)

quota_contract.per_delivery: Each DELIVERED event draws 1 unit via trackCallByKey (units defaults to 1 — byte-identical to pre-C4). The C1 units seam is unused on the webhook path.
quota_contract.note: top:N can fan one event to a subscriber whose coin set is large, but each DELIVERED event still costs exactly 1 (delivery-count, not coin-count).

consumers: GET /api/webhooks (serializeSubscription — assets echoed verbatim incl. tokens)
webhook delivery worker (rebuilds payload from allow-listed WebhookEventData)
drift_check_command: curl -s -X POST https://api.algovault.com/api/webhooks -H 'Authorization: Bearer <key>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"url":"https://example.com/h","events":["trade_call"],"assets":["top:25"]}' -w '%{http_code}\n'  # expect 201; assets:["top:101"] -> 400 invalid_asset_token
